root_text.sn55.43:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.43:1.4: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.43:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako paññavā hoti udayatthagāminiyā paññāya samannāgato ariyāya nibbedhikāya sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā.
root_text.sn55.43:3.3: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puññābhisandā kusalābhisandā sukhassāhārā.
root_text.sn55.43:4.1: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi puññābhisandehi kusalābhisandehi samannāgatassa ariyasāvakassa na sukaraṁ puññassa pamāṇaṁ gaṇetuṁ:
root_text.sn55.43:4.3: Atha kho asaṅkhyeyyo appameyyo mahāpuññakkhandhotveva saṅkhyaṁ gacchatī”ti.

translation_text.sn55.43:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of overflowing merit, overflowing goodness that nurture happiness.
translation_text.sn55.43:1.3: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.43:3.1: Furthermore, a noble disciple is wise. They have the wisdom of arising and passing away which is noble, penetrative, and leads to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.sn55.43:3.2: This is the fourth kind of overflowing merit, overflowing goodness that nurtures happiness.
translation_text.sn55.43:3.3: These are the four kinds of overflowing merit, overflowing goodness that nurture happiness.
translation_text.sn55.43:4.1: When a noble disciple has these four kinds of overflowing merit and goodness, it’s not easy to measure how much merit they have by saying that
translation_text.sn55.43:4.2: this is the extent of their overflowing merit, overflowing goodness that nurtures happiness.
translation_text.sn55.43:4.3: It’s simply reckoned as an incalculable, immeasurable, great mass of merit.”
translation_text.sn55.43:4.4: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn55.43:4.5: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn55.43:5.1: “One who desires merit, grounded in the skillful,
translation_text.sn55.43:5.2: develops the path to realize freedom from death.
translation_text.sn55.43:5.3: Once they’ve reached the heart of the teaching, <j>delighting in ending,
translation_text.sn55.43:5.4: they don’t tremble at the approach <j>of the King of Death.”

variant_text.sn55.43:5.4: maccurājāgamanasmin”ti → maccurājā gamissatīti (bj); maccurājāgamasminti (sya-all); maccurāja gamissatīti (pts1ed); maccujarākampisminti (mr)
